pos_exp_txt: Access to hiking trails and hiking adventure groups to meet new people. A network of immigrants from my own country and other countries. Job prospects with global businesses which can operate in English (this is a benefit to the province).
lifeeasier_qc_txt: Having access to public services in English. Important matters like health cannot be compromised. I speak some French, but after trying to explain a specific matter over the telephone in the past, the conversation went nowhere. So, I asked for English to get the issue solved, but they put the phone down on me. I called back with a French speaking friend and the matter was dealt with immediately. This is not pleasant.
othertopics_txt: Francisation language courses were beneficial. But there should be grater flexibility for those who work full time to participate in courses. Two hours per night, or one day per weekend. Otherwise you lose your work life balance, and become discouraged to learn.  Immigrants are strongly encouraged to join, but classes are oversubscribed.  Also, introduce a francisation programme eligibility for visitors who have applied for a work visa or have had one approved but haven't received it yet. While they apply for a job, this is an ideal time to learn the language. If they are in the system, they can pay back in future deductions if required. Ironically, when they begin working, they lose the time to benefit from the francisation programme.
topic_lang: The French language and its use in various life contexts
topic_culture: Quebec culture (films, music, books, arts, festivals...)
topic_cultshock: Culture shock and experiences of discrimination
topic_imminonrel: Relations between immigrants and people born in Quebec
